D6 Display L5 Evaluate Reference

Quantum Dot Emission

shrink the nanocrystal and its glow shifts blue — confinement tunes a razor-thin emission band that paints a wide gamut

A quantum dot is a semiconductor crystal a few nanometres across. Squeezing the electron into a smaller box raises its energy (quantum confinement), so a smaller dot emits a shorter, bluer wavelength. The emission band is extremely narrow — tens of nanometres — which is why QD displays reach such saturated, wide-gamut primaries.

Slide the dot diameter. The narrow emission peak slides from red (large dots) toward blue-green (small dots), and the swatch tracks it. Compare its width with the broad OLED bands — narrower means purer colour.
